Task / Use Case: Security audit of SAGE reviewed runtime and workflow files — reconnaissance, dependency coverage, code-pattern analysis, and infrastructure review
AI Usage Description: ema-security performed project-wide reconnaissance and targeted code-pattern review on the reviewed files plus related runtime surfaces and GitHub Actions workflows. Detected a static HTML/CSS/JavaScript site with no package manager manifests and two workflow files under .github/workflows. Verified npm was installed but no package.json or lockfile existed, so npm audit was not applicable; pip-audit, trivy, and grype were unavailable. Reviewed app.js, js/loader.js, js/renderer.js, js/url-state.js, index.html, config/sources.json, tests/sage-spec-requirements.mjs, and both workflows. Findings: 0 critical, 0 high, 1 medium (GitHub Pages workflow publishes the entire repository via path '.'), 2 low (missing explicit permissions block in copilot-setup-steps workflow; GitHub Actions pinned to mutable major tags instead of immutable SHAs). No code-level injection, XSS, auth, crypto, or secret-management vulnerabilities were found in the reviewed application code.
Outcome: Findings — 1 medium and 2 low in GitHub Actions configuration; reviewed application code had no code-level security vulnerabilities
Estimated Impact: Time saved ≈ 45-55% — AI handled reconnaissance, tool coverage checks, workflow inspection, and systematic pattern review across the runtime and CI surfaces quickly; developer still validates severity, decides public exposure tolerance for Pages content, and prioritizes workflow hardening (~1h AI-assisted vs ~2h fully manual)
